name: Cerrajón de Murtas — circular desde Murtas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Murtas offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de la Contraviesa, part of the Alpujarra region of Granada, Spain. The landscape features rolling scenery, extensive vineyards, and winding mountain passes, providing varied routes for mountain bikers. Trails often follow dirt tracks, old bridle paths, and dried riverbeds, with significant elevation changes. The region's natural features include the Cerrajón Peak, offering expansive vistas. Best mountain bike trails around Murtas The most popular mountain bike trail is Cerrajón de Murtas — circular desde Murtas, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends towards the Cerrajón Peak, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Circular Tierras del Oeste y Transnevada, a difficult 62.1 miles (100.0 km) path. This extensive route traverses varied terrain, including significant climbs and descents across the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Cádiar loop from Cádiar, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail leading through the Alpujarra landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Murtas is defined by rolling scenery, extensive vineyards, and winding mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging expeditions. The routes in Murtas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Murtas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Murtas?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Murtas, offering a variety of experiences within the Sierra de la Contraviesa. These routes range from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Murtas?
A: Mountain biking around Murtas features diverse terrain, including rolling scenery, extensive vineyards, and winding mountain passes. You'll encounter dirt tracks, old Roman and Moorish bridle paths, and dried riverbeds. The routes often involve significant elevation changes, with challenging ascents and picturesque descents through varied ecosystems, from lush valleys to more arid, high-altitude landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, many routes provide stunning panoramic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022083103/cerrajon-de-murtas-circular-desde-murtas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrajón de Murtas — circular desde Murtas&lt;/a&gt; trail ascends towards the Cerrajón Peak, which is the highest point in the Contraviesa. From here, you can enjoy expansive vistas encompassing the Sierra Nevada mountains to the north and the Mediterranean Sea to the south.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Murtas?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022083103/cerrajon-de-murtas-circular-desde-murtas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrajón de Murtas — circular desde Murtas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 16.2-mile (26.1 km) difficult trail. Another extensive circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190676428/circular-tierras-del-oeste-y-transnevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Tierras del Oeste y Transnevada&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 62.1 miles (100.0 km).

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Murtas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Murtas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Riders frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views, and the challenging nature of the routes, especially those leading to viewpoints like Cerrajón Peak.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Murtas?
A: The Alpujarra region, including Murtas, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making the challenging ascents more enjoyable and the landscapes vibrant with either spring blossoms or autumn colors. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter might bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes in Murtas are rated difficult, there are some moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yegen – Viewpoint Near Cáñar loop from Cádiar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.2-mile (22.8 km) trail that offers a less strenuous experience while still showcasing the region's beauty.

Q: What are some interesting places or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: The mountain biking routes often weave through or connect to charming Alpujarran villages, offering cultural immersion and inviting rest stops. Nearby attractions include the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4038646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bérchules&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yegen&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find geological wonders like the caves of La Gotera and La Vieja in the Cerrajón area, adding another layer of exploration to your trip.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190676428/circular-tierras-del-oeste-y-transnevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Tierras del Oeste y Transnevada&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This difficult route covers 62.1 miles (100.0 km) and involves substantial elevation changes, traversing varied terrain across the region.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Murtas?
A: Murtas and the surrounding Alpujarran villages offer various amenities. While specific cafes or accommodation directly on trails are not listed, these charming white villages provide opportunities for cultural immersion, traditional architecture, and local cuisine, serving as inviting rest stops and places to stay during extended rides.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes or challenging climbs in the area?
A: The Sierra de la Contraviesa is characterized by winding mountain passes that offer challenging climbs. Notable passes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mairena Hairpins&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nieles Switchbacks&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the diverse terrain that makes mountain biking in Murtas so engaging.
tips: At 1,500 meters above sea level, imposingly located in the municipality of Murtas, there is a wonderful viewpoint where you can enjoy impressive views of a large part of the Alpujarra. On clear days you can even see the coast of Africa.

In the Sierra de la Contraviesa and in the southeastern part of the Alpujarra of Granada is the small municipality of Murtas, right at the foot of its famous Cerrajón. Archaeological remains show human settlements from the Neolithic.
